text: based on what i have on file for mira, there are two things worth putting at the top of the sitter handoff: she has a **mild egg allergy** (hives), so check any snacks or foods for egg ingredients and have a plan for who to call if she reacts — her pediatric office number is a good thing to write down; and her **blue elephant** is her comfort object for naps, so making sure it's within reach at sleep time will make that transition much smoother for both of them. as a next step, you might jot those two things on a quick one-pager alongside your contact info, your pediatrician's number, and any nap schedule details — 18-month-olds do best when a new caregiver can mirror the usual routine as closely as possible, which helps keep separation anxiety low.
